term.label: investigation contentUrl
term.definition: URL or DOI link to the source publication for the investigation. Populate from the paper DOI as https://doi.org/{doi}.

term.label: study identifier
term.definition: Identifier of the PETase study, typically the DOI or internal study ID. This should match the Study row that owns the reaction observation units.

term.label: observation unit identifier
term.definition: Unique identifier for one PETase experimental subject under study, analogous to the individual, group, fermenter, or source-material object that receives treatments and is observed. For PETase studies this is usually an enzyme variant, substrate material, enzyme-substrate pair, enzyme panel, or substrate panel. Do not use this row for individual analytical measurements or for each reaction-condition readout.

term.label: observation unit type
term.definition: Type of PETase experimental subject. Controlled vocabulary: enzyme_variant | pet_substrate | proxy_substrate | enzyme_substrate_pair | enzyme_panel | substrate_panel | reaction_vessel | other.

term.label: experimental subject name
term.definition: Human-readable name for the PETase subject being observed, such as an enzyme variant, substrate material, or enzyme-substrate pair. Reaction conditions and analytical methods belong in Assay rows.

term.label: enzyme sample identifier
term.definition: Reference to the enzyme variant material used in this reaction ObservationUnit. Must match an enzyme identifier in the Sample sheet.

term.label: substrate sample identifier
term.definition: Reference to the PET or proxy substrate material used in this reaction ObservationUnit. Must match a substrate identifier in the Sample sheet.

term.label: reaction scale
term.definition: Experimental scale used during the assay/treatment applied to the PETase subject. Controlled vocabulary: microplate | flask | bottle | bioreactor | pilot_scale | industrial | not_reported | other.

term.label: reactor volume
term.definition: Total reaction volume or vessel scale for the assay/treatment condition. Keep the reported unit when litres, millilitres, or pilot-scale vessels are used.

term.label: enzyme loading
term.definition: Amount of enzyme applied in the assay/treatment condition. Preserve the reported basis, such as mg enzyme/g PET, micromolar enzyme, mg/mL, or mass fraction.

term.label: substrate solid loading
term.definition: Concentration or fraction of PET substrate in the assay/treatment condition, reported as g/L, % w/w, g/kg, or the paper-specific basis.

term.label: buffer type
term.definition: Buffer system or reaction medium used in the assay/treatment condition. Controlled vocabulary: potassium phosphate | sodium phosphate | glycine-NaOH | glycine-OH | Tris-HCl | HEPES | borate | water | phosphate | citrate | acetate | carbonate | other.

term.label: buffer concentration
term.definition: Molar concentration of the reaction buffer when reported.

term.label: reaction pH
term.definition: pH of the assay/treatment condition. If pH is controlled dynamically, record the set point and describe control in reaction condition notes.

term.label: reaction temperature
term.definition: Temperature at which the assay/treatment condition was conducted. Create separate Assay rows when the same PETase subject is tested at multiple temperatures.

term.label: agitation rate
term.definition: Shaking or stirring speed during the assay/treatment condition.

term.label: reaction time
term.definition: Duration of the assay/treatment condition. For time-series experiments, record the full time range here and individual measurement time points separately when available.

term.label: actual PET vs proxy substrate
term.definition: Whether this assay/treatment used real PET plastic, a soluble proxy substrate, or both. Controlled vocabulary: actual_pet | proxy_substrate | both | not_reported.

term.label: proxy substrate identity
term.definition: Identity of a soluble or fluorescent proxy substrate used instead of PET, such as BHET, MHET, pNPB, or BHET-OH. Leave empty for actual PET-only reactions.

term.label: depolymerisation measurement design
term.definition: Whether depolymerisation was followed as an endpoint, a time-resolved series, or both for this assay. Controlled vocabulary: endpoint | time_resolved | both | not_reported.

term.label: reaction condition notes
term.example: pH regulated by NaOH addition; 75 °C tested but detrimental due to recrystallization
term.definition: Additional reaction-system details that affect interpretation, including pH-stat control, non-isothermal runs, recrystallization notes, or paper-specific condition caveats.

term.label: sample type
term.definition: Type of material described in the Sample row. Controlled vocabulary: enzyme_variant | pet_substrate | proxy_substrate | reaction_aliquot | other.

term.label: enzyme identifier
term.definition: Unique identifier for one enzyme variant Sample row. Every wild type, engineered mutation combination, and comparator enzyme should receive its own row and identifier. Populated when sample type is enzyme_variant.

term.label: substrate identifier
term.definition: Unique identifier for one PET or proxy substrate Sample row. Every distinct material, supplier, physical form, crystallinity state, or preprocessing condition should receive its own row and identifier.

term.label: enzyme type
term.definition: Name or identifier of the PET hydrolase enzyme variant, including wild-type enzymes and engineered variants. Populated when sample type is enzyme_variant.

term.label: enzyme mutation
term.definition: Amino acid substitutions in the enzyme variant using standard notation. Multiple mutations should be separated by '/'. Populated when sample type is enzyme_variant.

term.label: enzyme organism source
term.definition: Source organism, engineered parent, or environmental library from which the enzyme scaffold was originally derived.

term.label: enzyme melting temperature
term.definition: Protein melting temperature (Tm) of the enzyme material. This is an enzyme property, not a reaction-condition or result row, and should be linked to the enzyme variant Sample.

term.label: enzyme melting temperature method
term.definition: Method used to determine enzyme melting temperature, such as differential scanning fluorimetry, circular dichroism, nanoDSF, or differential scanning calorimetry.

term.label: enzyme thermostability notes
term.example: Tm improved by +9.3 °C to +13.4 °C versus wild-type LCC
term.definition: Free-text enzyme thermostability details that cannot be represented as a single Tm value, such as relative increases, calcium effects, or stability rankings.

term.label: PET substrate type
term.definition: Type and origin of the PET or proxy substrate material. Include amorphous/crystalline state, commercial name, waste origin, or proxy substrate identity when reported.

term.label: substrate form factor
term.definition: Physical form of the PET substrate. Controlled vocabulary: powder | film | flake | nanoparticle | microplastic | fibre | pellet | preform | textile | soluble | other.

term.label: PET substrate source
term.definition: Supplier, commercial origin, waste-stream origin, bottle/preform source, or other provenance information for the substrate material.

term.label: PET substrate preprocessing
term.definition: Physical or thermal preprocessing applied to the PET substrate before enzymatic depolymerisation, including grinding, micronization, amorphization, washing, or thermal treatment.

term.label: PET crystallinity
term.definition: Percentage crystallinity of the PET substrate, usually measured by differential scanning calorimetry. This is a substrate material property and should be recorded on the substrate Sample row.

term.label: PET glass transition temperature
term.definition: Glass transition temperature (Tg) of the PET substrate material. Preserve first-heat and second-heat distinctions in notes when both are reported.

term.label: PET molecular weight
term.definition: Number-average or weight-average molecular weight of the PET substrate material, preserving whether Mn or Mw was reported.

term.label: assay identifier
term.definition: Unique identifier for one analytical measurement or analysis performed on a reaction ObservationUnit. Create separate Assay rows for distinct analytes, measurement time points, or analytical methods.

term.label: assay name
term.example: UHPLC quantification of TPA equivalents after 24 h
term.definition: Descriptive name of the analytical assay or measurement, not the whole reaction system.

term.label: observation unit identifier reference
term.definition: Reference to the reaction ObservationUnit measured by this Assay row. Must match an observation unit identifier in the ObservationUnit sheet.

term.label: assay type
term.definition: Type of analytical measurement. Controlled vocabulary: product_quantification | activity_screening | kinetic_parameter_estimation | thermal_stability | weight_loss | titration | other.

term.label: analytical separation method
term.definition: Chromatographic or other separation method used before detection. Record HPLC, UHPLC, LC-MS, no separation, or column details when reported.

term.label: detection method
term.definition: Detector or readout used to quantify products or activity. Controlled vocabulary: absorbance | fluorescence | refractive_index | mass_spectrometry | NaOH_titration | pH_stat | gravimetric | other.

term.label: UV detection wavelength
term.definition: UV wavelength used for absorbance-based detection. PET monomers are often detected near 240-242 nm, while EG may use another wavelength.

term.label: analyte identity
term.example: TPA | MHET | BHET | EG | total aromatic equivalents
term.definition: Identity of the analyte or product basis quantified by the Assay row. This is critical for comparing depolymerisation percentage, product concentration, and activity values.

term.label: measurement time point
term.definition: Time point at which the analytical measurement was taken, relative to reaction start. Required for time-resolved depolymerisation measurements.

term.label: PET depolymerisation percentage
term.definition: Percentage of PET substrate converted, solubilized, or degraded as reported by the analytical assay. Specify the analyte or calculation basis in analyte identity or measurement notes.

term.label: product concentration
term.definition: Concentration of depolymerisation products measured by the analytical assay, preserving the reported product basis and unit.

term.label: specific activity
term.definition: Specific activity derived from the measured reaction products per unit enzyme. Preserve the reported unit and product basis; use activity unit for normalization.

term.label: initial reaction rate
term.definition: Initial rate of product formation measured in the linear phase. This is not the same as space-time yield or endpoint depolymerisation percentage.

term.label: space-time yield
term.definition: Volumetric productivity of the reaction, expressed as product amount per volume per time. Keep enzyme-normalized and non-normalized variants distinguishable in the unit.

term.label: activity unit
term.definition: Canonical unit category for activity or productivity values. Use this to normalize the highly variable activity unit strings in expert annotations.

term.label: catalytic rate constant kcat
term.definition: Catalytic rate constant or turnover number. Rare for heterogeneous PET depolymerisation and usually meaningful only with the kinetic substrate basis and kinetic model.

term.label: Michaelis constant Km
term.definition: Michaelis-Menten constant. For PETase papers this is usually reported for soluble proxy substrates and should be interpreted with kinetic substrate basis and kinetic model.

term.label: kinetic substrate basis
term.definition: Substrate used to derive kinetic parameters. Km and kcat measured on BHET, MHET, pNPB, or PET films are not directly comparable without this field.

term.label: kinetic model
term.definition: Kinetic model used to derive the reported parameters, such as Michaelis-Menten, substrate inhibition, interfacial kinetics, or empirical linear-rate model.

term.label: half inactivation temperature
term.definition: Temperature at which the enzyme loses 50% of its initial activity after a defined incubation. Include incubation duration in measurement notes.

term.label: replicate count
term.definition: Number of independent or technical replicates for the analytical measurement.

term.label: measurement uncertainty
term.definition: Uncertainty or error of the measured value, including type such as SD, SEM, 95% CI, and replicate count when reported.

term.label: measurement notes
term.example: Activity calculated from NaOH consumption between 10% and 90% conversion
term.definition: Additional notes about analytical methodology, calculation approach, normalization, data quality, or why a kinetic value is not comparable across studies.
